significant source of employment for the tribe.  The plant is scheduled to be operational

economy and to reduce its reliance on coal.  The Navajo Nation has been working with 
the EPA and the New Mexico Public Service Commission to obtain a permit to

The couple met in the early 1930s when Allen was a young lawyer in
New York. Allen was a member of the Society of Jesus, a
Catholic order founded in 1848. Allen was a close friend of
Frances Marion Allen, the daughter of the founder of the order.

Allen and Marion married in 1936. Allen was a member of the
Order of the Holy Sepulchre, a Catholic religious order founded in
1891. The order is one of the oldest and most prestigious in the
United States.

The couple had two children, a son and a daughter. Allen died in
1958. Marion died in 1992.

The couple's home was in the Garden District of New York City.
